summary: ACCEPT. Independent read-only security review of release_engineer R2 found no exploitable Electron or release-scope regression. The reviewed diff is limited to allowed desktop/process paths; MONSOON_SMOKE_WEB only selects the packaged web entry for smoke evidence, normal desktop shell still loads the security shell, and MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY keeps priority. Electron remains nodeIntegration=false, contextIsolation=true, sandbox=true, webSecurity=true with CSP, renderer-root navigation restriction, denied window opens, schema-validated fullscreen IPC, and only desktop:getRuntimeInfo / desktop:setFullscreen exposed. Package smoke proves M5 packaged launch plus save/load/client-session boundary; import/export controls count 0 is accepted for this security review because no desktop file import/export bridge exists in the current slice and adding one would be new security surface.

tests_run.result: Clean before security handoff creation; branch chore/m5-web-windows-distributable.

Changed paths are apps/desktop/scripts/package-smoke.mjs, apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts, and allowed project task/message/state files only. No forbidden paths, dependency files, CI, gate docs, release manifests, content-source, sim-core, sim-ai, tools, telemetry, branding, signing, store, or commercial-release files changed.

Reviewed exact R2 code diff. main.ts adds MONSOON_SMOKE_WEB entry selection only when MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY is not set; package-smoke launches the packaged app with MONSOON_SMOKE_WEB=1 and asserts M5 workspace, runtime bridge, command preview/confirm, save, cancel, and load behavior.
Vite web production build passed; output includes dist/index.html and dist/host-hash-canary.html. Existing chunk-size warning is non-blocking.

Electron security preferences passed; preload exposes only monsoonDesktop; renderer file navigation is restricted to the packaged renderer root; allowed IPC channels are desktop:getRuntimeInfo and desktop:setFullscreen.
Fresh desktop smoke package generated at .tmp/desktop-package/MonsoonSovereigns-win32-x64 with main, preload, renderer, and platform adapter artifacts.

PASS. Packaged app launched M5 workspace in running phase, confirmed count 1, runtime bridge present, Save checkpoint wrote m5.save.client-session-written, Cancel/Load restored m5.load.client-session-restored. Import/export button count was 0 and logged as current-surface caveat.
Electron renderer dedicated worker matched Node baseline: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.
Without MONSOON_SMOKE_WEB, packaged app exposed the secure desktop shell once, M5 workspace count was 0, and bridge keys were getRuntimeInfo,setFullscreen.
In MONSOON_SMOKE_WEB mode, inline script injection was blocked by CSP, probe value stayed 0, and attempted navigation to https://example.com remained on the packaged file URL.

No arbitrary shell-open, dialog, path, filesystem bridge, net service, auto-updater, or extra Electron IPC surface found in the desktop/platform sources. Hits were the expected two IPC handlers, sandbox preload bridge, security/package scripts, and the unexposed platform type-level openExternal contract.

decisions: ACCEPT route_to=lead_orchestrator. Do not mark CLOSED in this security handoff; QA/release functional acceptance remains downstream.

The R2 diff is within allowed_paths. Product code changes are limited to apps/desktop/src/main/main.ts and apps/desktop/scripts/package-smoke.mjs; the remaining changes are allowed project task/message/routing state.

MONSOON_SMOKE_WEB is accepted as an evidence/test entry switch because it only changes the loaded local file entry to renderer/web/index.html for smoke mode. It does not add IPC, filesystem, shell-open, network, remote-code, or direct renderer authority.

MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY behavior is preserved because main.ts selects the smoke web entry only when smokeWebMode is true and hostHashCanaryMode is false. The Electron host-hash canary matched Node baseline after R2.

Electron security posture is accepted: nodeIntegration=false, contextIsolation=true, sandbox=true, webSecurity=true, allowRunningInsecureContent=false, strict CSP, denied window opens, renderer-root file navigation, and only desktop:getRuntimeInfo / desktop:setFullscreen IPC.

Import/export controls count 0 is not a blocker for this security review or for the current task's distributable boundary evidence. The current desktop bridge intentionally has no file import/export IPC; package smoke exercises the M5 client-session save/load serialization boundary and confirms absence of direct renderer filesystem access. Future desktop file import/export must be a separate reviewed bridge with allowlisted dialogs, path constraints, schema validation, malformed-input tests, and security evidence.

No telemetry, accounts, network services, store integration, installer signing, branding, commercial release, public release decision, arbitrary-code mod support, remote code execution, dependency change, license drift, compression/archive extraction surface, or malformed archive input surface was introduced.
risks: The package-smoke import/export locator count is 0 by current design. Functional QA should keep the known caveat visible: no explicit desktop file import/export bridge is proven in this slice.

If a later task adds desktop file import/export, this ACCEPT does not cover it; that work must add exact channel allowlists, path traversal defenses, malformed save/archive rejection tests, and renderer-safe IPC.

package-smoke inherits process.env. If a caller manually sets MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY=1 while running package-smoke, host-hash mode intentionally takes priority and the M5 smoke would fail rather than silently weakening the canary.
requested_action: Lead may integrate this ACCEPT security review and route next to qa_reviewer for functional validation; do not mark the task CLOSED until the required downstream acceptance is complete.
